skill.name: testing
skill.description: Guide for testing practices and frameworks
skill.summary: Guide for testing practices and frameworks
skill.author: 5t111111

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two command-related locations and three weak-cryptography locations. The weak-cryptography findings are false positives in ordinary prose, while line 24 is a legitimate test command that can execute project-defined test scripts. No evidence of malicious intent, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: External Test Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 24 instructs the agent to run `mise run test`. This is appropriate for a testing guide, but it can execute project-defined test scripts and should only be used in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present and intentionally executes local tests. The context is legitimate testing guidance, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Inline Filename Command
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 16 contains the inline filename extension `.test.ts`, not shell execution. There is no instruction or code path that executes this value.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a file naming convention inside documentation. No executable command or shell syntax is present on that line.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md lines 3, 8, and 19 contain documentation text about testing practices. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption code appears in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain English descriptions and naming guidance. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Test Runner Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends running `mise run test` to execute project tests. This is expected for a testing workflow, but users should understand that local test scripts may run arbitrary project code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and located in the running tests section. Its risk depends on the trustworthiness of the repository under test.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss edge cases because test structure is inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code direct guidance for Deno test files, coverage expectations, and test execution.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Deno's built-in testing framework for project tests.
Guides agents to place `.test.ts` files next to tested code.
Requires tests for public functions and public methods.
Encourages descriptive test names that state expected behavior.
Prompts coverage of edge cases and error handling.
Directs agents to run all tests with `mise run test`.
limitations: It does not include sample test code or reusable fixtures.
It assumes the project uses Deno and a configured mise test task.
It does not define coverage thresholds or reporting tools.
It does not review application-specific business logic by itself.

use_cases.title: Add Missing Unit Tests
use_cases.description: Create focused test files beside source files for public functions and methods.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Naming
use_cases.description: Review existing test names and make each case describe the behavior under test.

use_cases.title: Prepare Code For Review
use_cases.description: Check edge cases, error handling, and test execution before a pull request is submitted.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small function
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing skill to add Deno tests for the public functions in this file. Place each `.test.ts` file beside the code it tests.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an existing module
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this module and its tests. Add missing coverage for public methods, edge cases, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Test Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the current tests for naming clarity, public API coverage, edge case coverage, and failure-path coverage. Recommend the smallest useful changes.

prompt_templates.title: Run And Diagnose Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the project test command with `mise run test`, summarize failures, and propose targeted fixes without changing unrelated code.

output_examples.input: Add tests for a new parser utility.
output_examples.output: The agent creates a nearby test file, adds cases for valid input, invalid input, and boundary values, then recommends running the project test command.

output_examples.input: Review my current tests before a pull request.
output: Missing error-path and edge-case tests are listed.

output_examples.input: Tests are failing after a refactor.
output_examples.output: The agent runs the configured test command, groups failures by cause, and suggests focused fixes for the affected behavior.

best_practices: Keep test files beside the code they validate.
Write test names that clearly describe expected behavior.
Run the complete test command before committing changes.
anti_patterns: Do not skip tests for public functions or methods.
Do not rely only on happy-path test cases.
Do not run project commands in repositories you do not trust.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Deno's built-in testing framework.

faq.question: Where should test files be placed?
faq.answer: Place `.test.ts` files in the same directory as the code being tested.

faq.question: Does this skill require every private helper to have tests?
faq.answer: No. It specifically requires tests for public functions and public methods.

faq.question: How should tests be named?
faq.answer: Use descriptive names that clearly state the behavior or result being tested.

faq.question: How are tests run?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to run all project tests with `mise run test`.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the test command?
faq.answer: Only run it in trusted repositories because local test scripts can execute project code.
